channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Dr. Pimple Popper accepting new patients in Shreveport Bossier; here's how to apply
item.description: If you don't know who Dr. Sandra Lee / "Dr. Pimple Popper" is, you have not encountered the oddly therapeutic part of the internet of nasty cottage cheese cysts and large lipoma extractions.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, NewsNation

item.title: Fentanyl use up almost 300% in Baja California, health secretary says
item.description: "A lot of people are dying because of fentanyl, especially out on the streets, most times they can't get to hospitals in time to seek medical help.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court rules 4 Trump co-defendants can’t move Georgia election charges to federal court
item.description: An appeals court ruled Thursday that four people charged alongside former President Trump in his Georgia election racketeering case cannot move their charges from state to federal court.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Red-cockaded woodpeckers' recovery in southeast leads to status change from endangered to threatened
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The red-cockaded woodpecker, an iconic bird in southeastern forests, has recovered enough of its population to be downlisted from an endangered species to a threatened one, the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: CDC updates COVID-19 vaccine recommendations for older adults
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is urging some people to get additional doses of the COVID-19 vaccine for stronger protection against the virus.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: Georgia men who killed Ahmaud Arbery want a new trial
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — The only Black person who served on the jury that convicted three white men of murdering Ahmaud Arbery was called to the witness stand Thursday as defense attorneys sought to make a case that their clients deserve a new trial. The juror, identified in court only as No.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Like Biden, Harris puts focus on Trump as the end of the campaign draws near
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With the end of her abbreviated presidential campaign in sight, Kamala Harris is trying to put the focus squarely on Donald Trump and his threat to democratic institutions, echoing the strategy used by Joe Biden before he ended his reelection bid.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's lawyers seek dismissal of federal election case, call special counsel illegally appointed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Lawyers for former President Donald Trump told a federal judge Thursday that the election interference case against him should be dismissed, arguing that special counsel Jack Smith was illegally appointed and that funding for his office should be cut off.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Delphi murders: Defense seeks testimony from former Carroll County prosecutor
item.description: The motion said any information he had about the case would be “work product” and is not entitled to discovery.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: The 3 men convicted of murdering Ahmaud Arbery motion for new trial
item.description: Attorneys for the three convicted killers are making a range of arguments for a new trial, from a tainted jury to ineffective counsel for one of the men.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, NewsNation

item.title: President Biden to apologize for 150-year Indian boarding school policy
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — President Joe Biden said he will formally apologize on Friday for the country’s role in forcing Indigenous children for over 150 years into boarding schools, where many were physically, emotionally, and sexually abused and more than 950 died.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: In rare move, Pentagon denies falsehood about troops allowed to use force during election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a rare move, the Pentagon strongly pushed back Thursday against misinformation spread on social media that falsely suggests U.S. troops have been authorized to use force against American citizens during the election.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Canada will reduce immigration targets as Trudeau acknowledges his policy failed
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Thursday that Canada will significantly reduce the number of new immigrants it allows into the country after acknowledging that his government failed to get the balance right coming out of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: EPA imposes stricter standards to protect children from exposure to lead paint
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two weeks after setting a nationwide deadline for removal of lead pipes, the Biden administration is imposing strict new limits on dust from lead-based paint in older homes and child-care facilities.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: USPS mailbox in Arizona set on fire, damaging several ballots
item.description: Phoenix police reported that the suspect said the arson was not politically motivated and that he just wanted to be arrested, according to multiple outlets.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KRON4

item.title: A cub of famous Wyoming grizzly No. 399 has been unseen since his mom's death but odds look good
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — The death of the world’s most famous grizzly bear on a highway in western Wyoming has made an orphan of its cub, but biologists say the youngster’s chances of surviving -- even with a chilly mountain winter coming on -- are good.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald's E. coli outbreak: Why only the Quarter Pounder was linked
item.description: McDonald's has voluntarily stopped selling its Quarter Pounder hamburger at restaurants across Colorado and other states after an E. coli outbreak was linked to the menu item.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutors seek resentencing for Erik and Lyle Menendez in 1989 killings of their parents
item.description: Prosecutors are recommending that Erik and Lyle Menendez be resentenced for the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills.
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CDC recommends lower age for people to get vaccine against pneumonia
item.description: All adults aged 50 and older should receive a vaccine against bacteria that can cause pneumonia and meningitis, federal health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The 3 men convicted of murdering Ahmaud Arbery motion for new trial
item.description: Travis and Gregory McMichael armed themselves with guns and jumped in a pickup truck to chase Arbery, a 25-year-old Black man, after they saw him run past their house on Feb. 23, 2020.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, KRON4

item.title: Should elephants have the same rights as people? A court in Colorado may decide
item.description: Colorado's highest court will hear arguments on whether the African female elephants should be legally able to challenge their captivity under a long-held process used by prisoners to dispute their detention.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Colleges left helpless as students rule out schools due to state politics
item.description: Colleges and universities find themselves increasingly trapped with the politics of the state where they reside.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: Evangelicals emerge as a potent lobby for US support of Ukraine
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Since the war in Ukraine began, Yaroslav Pyzh, a Baptist pastor in Lviv, has worn many hats. He runs the large and growing Ukrainian Baptist Theological Seminary, while overseeing a network of 18 humanitarian aid centers across the embattled country.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris grilled for backpedaling on calling the border wall ‘stupid’ and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in shootings of Democratic office in Arizona had over 120 guns in his home, officials find
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — An Arizona prosecutor said the man arrested in the shooting of a Democratic National Committee office in suburban Phoenix had more than 120 guns and over 250,000 rounds of ammunition in his home, leading law enforcement to believe he may have been planning a mass casualty event.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW and WALTER BERRY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Letter at center of Menendez brothers' bid for freedom called into question
item.description: Kitty Menendez's brother questions letter at heart of Lyle and Erik Menendez's appeal for freedom despite life without parole for shotgun murder of their parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tim Burton talks about his dread of AI as an exhibition of his work opens in London
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The imagination of Tim Burton has produced ghosts and ghouls, Martians, monsters and misfits — all on display at an exhibition that is opening in London just in time for Halloween. But you know what really scares him? Artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Beloved Grand Teton grizzly bear No. 399 fatally struck by a vehicle in Wyoming
item.description: Grand Teton National Park’s famous grizzly bear No. 399, known by wildlife photographers around the world, died after being struck by a vehicle Tuesday in Wyoming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Should elephants have the same rights as people? A Colorado court may decide
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Missy, Kimba, Lucky, LouLou, and Jambo have lived in Colorado Springs for decades in the elephant exhibit at the Cheyenne Mountain Zoo.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners accused of stashing dead bodies plead guilty to federal fraud charges
item.description: Colorado funeral home owners accused of misspending nearly $900,000 in COVID-19 pandemic relief funds have pleaded guilty Thursday to federal charges for defrauding customers.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: California prestigious private school sued after expelling boy, 10, for using squirt gun emoji, rap lyrics
item.description: The parents of 10-year-old student at a Los Angeles private school are suing the school after the boy was expelled for sending emails containing squirt gun emojis and rap lyrics to another student.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Kamala Harris wrong about Trump's Hitler comments made to Kelly
item.description: "They (Trump's comments) don't mean anything, and he doesn't even remember them 30 seconds later, O'Reilly said on "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump: Glad Donald Trump is endorsing early, mail-in voting
item.description: She says her father-in-law "understands how important this is to making sure that he wins on election night.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia, candidates target of ‘sustained’ cyberattacks: Reports
item.description: CNN says Georgia fended off the likely overseas attack, while Microsoft says China, Russia and Iran are targeting the U.S. election process.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Ron Ely, TV's 'Tarzan' in the 1960s, dies at 86
item.description: Ron Ely, the tall, musclebound actor who played the title character in the 1960s NBC series “Tarzan,” has died at age 86.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida mailman stabs lawncare worker over parking: deputies
item.description: The Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office has released more information about the events that may have led up to a deadly stabbing Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KRON4

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4
